source_receipts.reading: PIDFF set-effect, set-constant-force, effect-start, and final Stop All were accepted as a bounded low-torque proof.
The bounded shaped visible-motion profile wrote successfully and measured 0.18127718013275285 degrees, below the 1 degree visible threshold.
The first 1 degree controlled-angle attempt sent five PIDFF writes and timed out at 0.18127718013275285 degrees.
The retry sent 33 PIDFF writes using bounded-pidff-micro-step-v2 and remained in the same 0.18127718013275285 degree response band.

low_torque: 0x01 set effect block 1 constant duration 150 ms gain 3 direction 9000
0x0A effect operation start block 1 loop count 1
first_controlled_angle: 0x01 set effect block 1 constant duration 2000 ms gain 13 direction 9000
0x0A effect operation start block 1 loop count 1
0x0C device control Stop All after outbound attempt
retry_micro_step_v2: 8 outbound repetitions of set effect duration 250 ms, set constant force magnitude 1638, effect start loop count 1, then Stop All
no return-leg PIDFF writes because the outbound 1 degree target was never reached

likely_semantics_questions: whether block index 1 is sufficient without an explicit Create New Effect / Block Load lifecycle on this R5 state
whether Stop All after every 250 ms micro-step prevents accumulation of visible steering displacement
whether the device expects actuator enable, device gain, or effect restart ordering before constant force updates have authority
whether direction, gain, duration, or loop count is interpreted differently by the R5 firmware than the current descriptor-derived assumption

required_no_output_next_work: design a software-only effect-lifecycle plan before any third output attempt
evaluate whether a future profile should test a different PIDFF operation order, effect lifecycle, gain/device-control setup, or block allocation strategy
keep target_degrees=1, max_percent=5, and timeout_ms<=2000 unless a separate safety review explicitly changes them
record a new reviewed plan artifact with planned_next_output.allowed=false before any future authorization
require fresh command-bound bench clear and exact authorization only after the software plan lands
forbidden_next_steps: Pit House or SimHub as native prerequisites
using passive sniff artifacts as native readiness evidence
notes: This artifact is no-output diagnosis only. It did not open a HID device and did not send output, feature, serial, firmware, or DFU commands.
The retry evidence suggests that replaying the same 5 percent constant-force PIDFF lifecycle more often did not increase steering displacement.
Native visible motion remains unproven until a later reviewed and authorized receipt reaches the visible-motion threshold and satisfies the verifier.
